title: Never commit secrets to source
description: Keep credentials out of tracked files entirely, sourcing them from the environment or a secret manager so they never enter version control.
body: Use whenever code needs a credential, token, or key. Do not paste a secret into source, config, or a tracked .env to make something work. Actions: read secrets from environment variables or a secret manager at runtime; add credential file patterns to .gitignore; provide a committed example file with placeholder values only. Validation signals include a scan finding no secrets in the tree and the app resolving credentials at runtime from the manager. Risk boundary: a secret committed even once is compromised and must be rotated, so deny any change that hardcodes one and escalate if a real value is found.
